message:
refactor: phased startup lifecycle, child counters, ID collision retry, and storage hardening

This is a broad robustness overhaul touching config resolution, the main
entrypoint lifecycle, storage operations, issue creation, and sync.

## Config: Eliminate redundant IO via StartupConfig

The old code loaded startup config, resolved paths, and opened storage
independently in open_storage(), open_storage_with_cli(), load_config(),
resolve_paths(), run_auto_import(), and run_auto_flush() — each doing its
own config::load_startup_config() + ConfigPaths::resolve() calls. This
meant 3-6 redundant filesystem reads per command invocation.

Introduce StartupConfig struct and load_startup_config_with_paths() to
load all startup layers and resolve paths exactly once, threading the
result through all consumers. This also fixes a subtle JSONL path
priority bug: metadata.json's explicit jsonl_export override now takes
precedence over DB-override-derived sibling paths (Priority 2 before
Priority 3), so workspaces with custom JSONL names don't silently fall
back to issues.jsonl when a --db flag is also used.

Also expand should_attempt_jsonl_recovery() to recognize TableExists,
IndexExists, and "malformed database schema ... already exists" errors
from duplicate sqlite_master entries — a corruption mode seen in
production after WAL-mode crashes where blocked_issues_cache gets
double-registered.

## main.rs: 5-Phase Startup Lifecycle

Replace the scattered run_auto_import() and run_auto_flush() functions
with a structured 5-phase pipeline:

  1. Startup & Discovery — resolve beads dir, load config, resolve paths
  2. Open Storage — single connection, only when needed
  3. Auto-Import — reuse existing storage handle
  4. Command Execution — unchanged
  5. Auto-Flush — reuse existing storage handle

The old design opened storage up to 3 times per command (auto-import,
command, auto-flush). The new design opens it at most once and passes
the handle through. StartupContext encapsulates the bootstrap state and
provides no_db(), no_auto_import(), no_auto_flush() accessors.

## Storage: Child Counters, Dedup Guards, and Transactional Safety

- Add child_counters table support: rebuild_child_counters_in_tx()
  scans all hierarchical IDs to populate parent_id -> last_child
  mappings; update_child_counter_in_tx() maintains them incrementally
  on create. next_child_number() now checks child_counters first
  (O(1) lookup) before falling back to the LIKE-scan.

- Deduplicate labels and dependencies on insert: multiple code paths
  (create_issue, replace_labels, replace_dependencies) now use
  HashSet-based seen trackers to silently skip duplicates instead of
  hitting constraint errors. This matters because JSONL imports can
  contain duplicate entries from compaction artifacts.

- Replace UniqueViolation on create with IdCollision error: the old
  error type was a FrankenError variant that couldn't carry context.
  The new BeadsError::IdCollision { id } enables structured retry.

- Wrap set_metadata() in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT with ROLLBACK on
  failure — the old bare DELETE + INSERT could leave metadata in a
  half-written state if the INSERT failed.

- delete_issue() now cleans up events, dirty_issues, export_hashes,
  blocked_issues_cache, and child_counters (previously only cleaned
  labels, dependencies, comments, and the issue row itself). Also
  sets ctx.force_flush = true so the deletion propagates to JSONL.

- Add force_flush mechanism: MutationContext gains a force_flush flag
  that, when set, writes needs_flush=true to metadata. Auto-flush
  checks this flag alongside dirty_count. finalize_export clears it.

- Tombstone status handling in update_issue: properly sets deleted_at,
  deleted_by, delete_reason fields; reopening clears them.

- Add LIMIT support to list_issues() query builder.

## Create: ID Collision Retry with Backoff

Both create_issue_impl() and execute_import() now wrap the create call
in a retry loop (up to 10 attempts, 10ms * attempt backoff). This
handles the race where concurrent agents generate the same hash-based
ID from similar inputs. The generate_new_id() helper is extracted to
share logic between single-create and batch-import paths.

## Sync: PID-scoped Temp Files and Staleness Improvements

- Temp files now include PID in filename (e.g., issues.jsonl.12345.tmp)
  to prevent collisions when multiple br processes run concurrently.
  Applies to export, sync, history restore, and base snapshot save.

- compute_staleness() now considers both last_import_time AND
  last_export_time when deciding if JSONL is newer. Previously, a
  successful export didn't update the staleness baseline, causing
  unnecessary re-imports on the next command.

- merge_issue() short-circuits when left and right have identical
  content_hash — avoids expensive field-by-field comparison.

- import_from_jsonl() and execute_merge() call
  rebuild_child_counters_in_tx() to ensure counters stay consistent
  after bulk data changes.

- ConflictResolution now derives clap::ValueEnum + serde traits in
  preparation for CLI-configurable merge strategy.

## Minor Fixes

- audit.rs: Tolerate malformed audit log entries (warn + skip instead
  of hard error) so a single corrupt line doesn't break the audit
  trail.

- changelog.rs: Fix output mode — --robot flag now correctly bypasses
  Rich/text rendering.

- history.rs: Fix TOCTOU race on restore by using PID-scoped temp
  files and tolerating ENOENT on remove_file.

- stats.rs: Replace spawn + piped stdout with simpler Command::output()
  for git log subprocess.

- sync/history.rs: Tolerate ENOENT in remove_history_artifact_if_present
  to prevent races between concurrent cleanup operations.

- config: Add "display.*" to is_startup_key() so display settings are
  available during early bootstrap (before DB is opened).

- config: Fix doc comment numbering for color precedence.

- Housekeeping: Remove 5 stale .br_history snapshots and update
  issues.jsonl to reflect current issue state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
